questions.text: People have wondered for a long time how their personalities and behaviors are formed. Two distinct school of thoughts on the matter have developed. Those who support the nature side of the conflict believe that our personalities and behavior patterns are largely determined by biological and genetic factors that environment has little to do with our abilities, characteristics, and behavior is central to this theory. Proponents of the nurture theory claim that our environment is more important than our biologically based instincts in determining how we will act. They see humans as beings whose behavior is almost completely shipped by their surroundings. Neither of these studies can yet fully explain human behavior.
